item.title: Russian strikes kill 19 across Ukraine
item.description: A massive Russian drone and missile barrage killed at least 19 people in cities across Ukraine, more than four years into the war with talks on ending the conflict stalled....

item.title: Jamaat-led alliance to hold rally in Dhaka tomorrow demanding implementation
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance will hold a rally in the capital Dhaka tomorrow if the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government does not implement the referendum verdict....Geographic Reference

item.title: Hearing in Tulip’s Gulshan flat scam case May 6
item.description: A Dhaka court on Thursday set May 6 for holding a charge-framing hearing in a case filed against Tulip Siddiq, niece of ousted prime minister Sheikh Hasina, and former assistant legal adviser of Rajdhani Unnayan Kartripakkha Sardar Mosharraf Hossain for......

item.title: Palak ordered July internet shutdown, BTRC officer tells ICT
item.description: A Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission officer on Thursday testified before the International Crimes Tribunal-1 that former ICT state minister Zunaid Ahmed Palak, who is now detained in jail, ordered a nationwide internet shutdown from......

item.title: 43pc households lack internet access: BBS
item.description: Around 43 per cent of the country’s households remains without internet access despite more than a decade of rapid expansion in online and smartphone-based services....

item.title: Govt prioritises peace, security in public life, says PM
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday said that his government is prioritising restoring peace and security in public life by improving law and order and strictly controlling corruption....

item.title: 6 more children with measles symptoms die
item.description: At least six more children with measles symptoms died, and 1,191 other suspected cases were detected in the 24 hours until 8:00am on Thursday, according to the Directorate General of Health Services....

item.title: Mujibnagar Day today
item.description: Today marks the historic Mujibnagar Day, commemorating the oath-taking ceremony of the newly independent Bangladesh’s provisional government on April 17, 1971....

item.title: Fuel pump schedules go haywire
item.description: Consumers’ woes have continued to intensify as the schedules of most fuel pumps in the city have gone haywire....

item.title: Govt’s domestic debt Tk 11 lakh crore amid limited revenues
item.description: Bangladesh government’s domestic borrowing reached nearly Tk 11 lakh crore in January, reflecting a sharp rise in reliance on internal sources to finance budget deficits and signalling mounting pressure from debt servicing costs......

item.title: Forex reserve crosses $35b again
item.description: Bangladesh’s gross foreign exchange reserves crossed $35 billion again on Thursday, supported by strong remittance inflows and sustained dollar purchases by Bangladesh Bank......

item.title: Govt targets 10,000MW solar power by 2030
item.description: The government has set a target to generate 10,000 megawatts of electricity from solar power by 2030 as part of a broad strategy to ease the country’s ongoing energy crisis and accelerate the transition to renewable energy....

item.title: IFC, JPMorgan want to invest in Bangladesh: Khosru
item.description: Finance minister Amir Khosru Mahmud Chowdhury on Wednesday said that global financial institutions, including International Finance Corporation and JPMorgan, had shown interest in investing in Bangladesh, focusing on the financial sector and capital market......

item.title: Heritage, innovation in spotlight at BSCIC Baishakhi fair
item.description: The BSCIC Baishakhi Fair at Bangla Academy is bringing together young and traditional entrepreneurs, showcasing a diverse range of handcraft, heritage and sustainable products while highlighting both creativity and market challenges......

item.title: RNPP likely to go on operation in late summer
item.description: The under-construction Roopur Nuclear Power Plant has obtained commissioning licence and is expected to go on operation in late summer in the power-thirsty country, said officials of the Ministry of Science and Technology on Thursday....

item.title: IMF warns of ‘tough times’ if oil prices stay high
item.description: IMF chief Kristalina Georgieva warned Wednesday of difficult times ahead for the global economy if war in the Middle East is unresolved and oil prices stay high, adding that inflation risks could seep into food prices......

item.title: Agent banking expands access, but gaps remain, study finds
item.description: Bangladesh’s agent banking revolution has a paradox at its heart; over the past decade, the country has built one of South Asia’s most extensive agent banking networks over 21,000 outlets stretching from the river deltas of Barishal to the highland fringes of the Chittagong Hill Tracts, twenty-four million accounts have been opened, the majority by people in rural areas who had never held a bank account in their lives......

item.title: Cotton farming boom transforms agriculture in Chuadanga
item.description: Cotton cultivation is expanding rapidly in Chuadanga, marking a notable shift in the district’s agricultural landscape as farmers increasingly turn to the ‘white gold’ cash crop amid rising demand, favourable weather and attractive market prices......

item.title: PM visits BADC stall at Tangail Agricultural Fair
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman visited the stall of the Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation on the inaugural day of a two-day Agricultural Fair at the Pauro Udyan in Tangail on Tuesday....

item.title: Bangladesh’s long-term energy strategy
item.description: AT PRESENT, the world is in a state of severe geopolitical instability. Even before the conflicts between Israel and Palestine and between Ukraine and Russia could be resolved, a large-scale Iran–Israel confrontation intensified. The United States’ direct support for Israel, along with the indirect backing of Iran by Russia, China, and France, has significantly altered the......

item.title: Food security and nutrition crisis
item.description: WE HAVE been accustomed to treating wars as distant geopolitical events — horrific for those in the crossfire but contained. The 2026 Iran war shatters that illusion. This is not merely a battle over territory or political influence; it is a systemic shock to the global food system that will raise the price of our next plate of food and, more critically, push millions of people, including children, towards starvation.......

item.title: Making Dhaka liveable
item.description: UNLIKE Canberra, the seat of the government and then the capital city of Australia, established as centrally drawn and designed by the national capital commission committee headed by Sir john Overall in 1958–72, Dhaka did not commence its journey being built on a clean slate. It, therefore, cared for neither the greenery to retain them as lungs for dwellers nor the......

item.title: Teacher deficit and learning crisis
item.description: BANGLADESH has never lacked promises about education. Successive governments have treated it as a priority sector, rolling out free textbooks, stipends, and school feeding programmes with regularity. Enrolment rates have increased, attendance has improved, and public examination results often suggest steady progress. These indicators are frequently......

item.title: Dhaka’s dying canals demand urgent action
item.description: The deplorable condition of Shyampur Canal, as a photograph that New Age published on April 16 shows, is reflective of the sorry state of the canals and water bodies in and around Dhaka. Garbage, mostly polythene waste, has made the canal stagnant, making it a breeding ground for mosquitoes, and turned black the water, which emits a foul odour. The state of other water......

item.title: Govt needs to act early to head off fertiliser crisis
item.description: A SHORTAGE of urea fertilisers, as the situation on the ground and people involved in the sector suggest, looms large, which may harm the production of aman rice, the second largest rice crop after boro, which is planted in July–August and harvested in November–December. All this may happen because of disruption in urea production. The import of fertiliser remains stalled......

item.title: Dhaka, Beijing eye stronger ties in media
item.description: Bangladesh and China on Thursday held in-depth exchanges on strengthening cooperation in news media, film, and television between the two nations....

item.title: KILLING OF PIR IN MOB ATTACK: None arrested in five days
item.description: None of the suspects in the case of the killing of pir Abdur Rahman in a mob attack on his durbar sharif in Daulatpur upazila of Kushtia has been arrested even five days after the murder....

item.title: MRDI to organise 2-day journalism conference in May
item.description: Media Resources Development Initiative on Thursday announced that the media development organisation would organise a two-day journalism conference in May in Dhaka addressing the challenges of independent journalism and planning for the future....Geographic Reference

item.title: Minor child hacked to death at Shahzadpur
item.description: A MINOR child, Mahtab Hossain Hridoy, 5, was allegedly hacked to death by his paternal uncles in Shahzadpur upazila of Sirajganj on Thursday morning....

item.title: Khalilur leaves for Türkiye to attend diplomacy forum
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman on Thursday morning left the country for Türkiye to attend the Antalya Diplomacy Forum....

item.title: India plans parliament expansion for women
item.description: India’s government was seeking to expand the size of parliament by two-fifths to increase representation of women lawmakers, but opposition parties have cried foul, saying it would benefit the ruling party....

item.title: Abdun Naser appointed secretary to textiles and jute ministry on contract
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government on Thursday appointed Md Abdun Naser Khan as secretary to the textiles and jute ministry on a contractual basis for one year....

item.title: 63 HC benches formed after vacation
item.description: Sixty-three High Court benches were formed on Thursday for conducting judicial proceedings after the end of the vacation....

item.title: Medical board formed at RMCH as suspected measles deaths reach 51
item.description: Rajshahi Medical College Hospital authorities on Thursday formed an eight-member medical board to strengthen treatment and management of measles and its complications as 51 children with measles symptoms have so far died in Rajshahi amid a......

item.title: Chinmoy Krishna Das granted bail in one case
item.description: Chinmoy Krishna Das, the suspended ISKCON leader and spokesperson of Bangladesh Sammilito Sanatani Jagran Jote, has been granted bail in one case on Thursday while remaining in custody in Chattogram jail....

item.title: Six put on remand in youth gang leader murder case
item.description: A Dhaka metropolitan magistrate court on Thursday allowed the police to take six detained accused for four days in their custody for interrogation in the case of April 12 killing of youth gang leader Emon in the capital’s Mohammadpur area....

item.title: Factory worker’s body recovered in Gazipur
item.description: The police on Thursday recovered the body of a factory worker from beside the Dhaka-Tangail Highway in Gazipur’s Kaliakair area....

item.title: Chamber judge clears way for NTRCA teachers recruitment exam
item.description: A chamber judge of the Appellate Division on Thursday stayed a High Court order that had partially stayed a recruitment examination for no-government head teachers and assistant head teachers....

item.title: Counterfeit certificates of 471 non-govt teachers detected
item.description: The Directorate of Inspection and Audit has recently detected the counterfeit certificates of 471 teachers of different non-government schools and colleges....

item.title: Juba Dal leader among two held over Dhamrai police attack
item.description: A local Jatiyatabadi Jubo Dal leader was among two people arrested after police were attacked twice during a raid to detain suspects in a pornography and violence against women case at Dhamrai upazila in Dhaka, officials said....Geographic Reference

item.title: Ex-DGFI official Manjil remanded again in July case
item.description: A Dhaka court on Thursday remanded former Directorate General of Forces Intelligence official retired Major Manjil Haider Chowdhury for the third time in a case lodged over the killing of hawker Abdul Wadud during the July mass uprising....

item.title: Info minister pledges full support for women journalists
item.description: Information and broadcasting minister Zahir Uddin Swapon on Thursday assured all kinds of support for the development and flourishing of women journalists as a delegation from the Bangladesh Women Journalists Forum called on him at his office in Dhaka....

item.title: Establishment of rights for students with disabilities at DU urged
item.description: Dhaka University Support for Students with Disabilities on Thursday submitted a memorandum to DU vice-chancellor, ABM Obaidul Islam, urging stronger measures to ensure the rights, welfare and inclusion of students with disabilities on campus....

item.title: 10 ‘journalists’ sued for demanding extortion
item.description: Dr Rafsanjani, a medical officer at Monirampur Upazila Health Complex, filed a case on Wednesday with the court of Senior Judicial Magistrate Jakia Sultana, accusing 10 individuals of demanding extortion as journalists, as confirmed by Abdur Rahman Sohagh, the plaintiff’s lawyer....

item.title: Fuel crisis, power outages put boro cultivation at risk in northern region
item.description: Farmers across the country’s northern region are becoming increasingly anxious over their standing boro paddy as a deepening fuel crisis and frequent power outages are disrupting irrigation amid a heatwave sweeping over the region....

item.title: One held over girl’s rape in Narsingdi
item.description: Police arrested a man in connection with the rape of a young girl at Saner Bari village on Palash upazila of Narsingdi on Thursday....

item.title: BGB opens new BOP in Dinajpur’s Biral
item.description: Border Guard Bangladesh has inaugurated a new Border Out Post in the border area of Mouchosa under Biral upazila in Dinajpur, aiming to enhance operational capacity in preventing smuggling and other cross-border crimes....

item.title: 279 held in week-long river police drive
item.description: In a nationwide crackdown aimed at protecting fisheries and ensuring security on waterways, the river police conducted a series of drive over the past seven days, leading to the arrest of 279 individuals and the seizure of a large quantity of illegal fishing......

item.title: Lightning kills six in four districts
item.description: At least six people were killed in separate incidents of lightning strikes in Chuadanga, Jashore, Jhenaidah and Munshiganj districts on Thursday....

item.title: MFPS honours Professor SMA Faiz
item.description: The Mahanambrata Foundation for Peace and Solidarity, established in the department of world religions and culture, Dhaka University, has awarded former University Grants Commission of Bangladesh chairman Professor SMA Faiz....
